evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: measure and interpret the experiments that decide what we ship
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Shipping/experimentation velocity language is balanced by ownership of trusted metrics and modelling, giving rough parity.
evidence.domain_risk: Dig into behavioural and financial data to find user segments
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Consumer wellness product with some financial/revenue analysis but no regulatory or compliance language, placing it at moderate risk.
evidence.data_team_maturity: directly mentoring and developing other analytics engineers
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named tools (BigQuery, Looker, dbt) appear in responsibilities and mentoring of other analytics engineers implies a growing team, but no distinct sub-teams are named.
evidence.jd_authorship: Strong SQL on a modern cloud warehouse. We use BigQuery, Looker and dbt
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific tool stack and concrete responsibility descriptions (A/B test analysis, semantic layers, agentic analytics) give a clear picture of daily work.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Turn questions from the product team and company leaders into clear, useful answers
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Primary partner named is the product team, with A/B testing and feature shipping as the core work, indicating product orientation.
evidence.autonomy_level: Own how data comes in, how it's modelled, and the metrics people trust
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Ownership verbs ("own", "build the data models... the whole company relies on") plus mentoring/leadership signal a strategic, direction-setting role.
evidence.ai_role: Build the tooling, semantic layers and agents that let the rest of the company answer their own questions
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate builds agentic analytics infrastructure that AI agents run on, making AI downstream of the candidate's output.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: No explicit testing, data contracts, or data quality framework language appears anywhere in the extracted JD text.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: The JD is framed entirely in delivery, ownership, and capability terms with no risk or compliance register.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Build the tooling, semantic layers and agents that let the rest of the company answer their own questions
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Mix of building new self-serve/agentic tooling and owning/maintaining existing data ingestion and modelling.
